initial_test_output:
Server listening on port 18084
Sending 15 requests without header spoofing (limit is 10)...
Without spoofing: 10 passed, 5 blocked
PASS: Rate limiter correctly blocked excess requests
Sending 30 requests with rotating X-Forwarded-For header (limit is 10)...
With header spoofing: 30 passed, 0 blocked
FAIL: Rate limiter bypassed — 30 of 30 requests passed with spoofed X-Forwarded-For
      Attacker can rotate header to get unlimited requests

cmd: sed -i 's/const forwarded = req.headers\[\'x-forwarded-for\'];/    const forwarded = undefined;/' /app/app/ratelimiter.ts
output_preview: bash: -c: line 1: unexpected EOF while looking for matching `''
bash: -c: line 1: unexpected EOF while looking for matching `''

sed: unmatched '/'
bash: -c: line 3: unexpected EOF while looking for matching `''

bash: -c: line 2: syntax error near unexpected token `('
bash: -c: line 2: `  getClientIp(req: http.IncomingMessage): string {\'

cmd: awk '/getClientIp\(/ { found=1; print "  getClientIp(req: http.IncomingMessage): string {"; print "    // Always use the real remote address, ignoring X-Forwarded-For to prevent spoofing"; print "    return req.socket.remoteAddress || \'127.0.0.1\';"; print "  }"; next } found && /^  \}/ { found=0; next } !found { print }' /app/app/ratelimiter.ts > /tmp/ratelimiter.ts && mv /tmp/ratelimiter.ts /app/app/ratelimiter.ts
output_preview: bash: -c: line 1: unexpected EOF while looking for matching `"'
